rerun0.24.0-alpha.1+dev

In rerun::external::re_renderer::external::bytemuck::__core::arch::x86_64

rerun::external::re_renderer::external::bytemuck::__core::arch::x86_64

Function _mm_mask_fmadd_round_sh

Source 
pub unsafe fn _mm_mask_fmadd_round_sh<const ROUNDING: i32>(
    a: __m128h,
    k: u8,
    b: __m128h,
    c: __m128h,
) -> __m128h
🔬This is a nightly-only experimental API. (stdarch_x86_avx512_f16)
Available on x86-64 only.
Expand description

Multiply the lower half-precision (16-bit) floating-point elements in a and b, and add the intermediate result to the lower element in c. Store the result in the lower element of dst using writemask k (the element is copied from a when the mask bit 0 is not set), and copy the upper 7 packed elements from a to the upper elements of dst.

Rounding is done according to the rounding parameter, which can be one of:

  • _MM_FROUND_TO_NEAREST_INT | _MM_FROUND_NO_EXC : round to nearest and suppress exceptions
  • _MM_FROUND_TO_NEG_INF | _MM_FROUND_NO_EXC : round down and suppress exceptions
  • _MM_FROUND_TO_POS_INF | _MM_FROUND_NO_EXC : round up and suppress exceptions
  • _MM_FROUND_TO_ZERO | _MM_FROUND_NO_EXC : truncate and suppress exceptions
  • _MM_FROUND_CUR_DIRECTION : use MXCSR.RC - see _MM_SET_ROUNDING_MODE

Intel’s documentation
